jokes.text: This one keeps eating himself...
jokes.setup: Alien dinner party scene with multiple octopuses as food
jokes.punchline: One octopus is literally consuming itself in a loop
jokes.explanation: Strong absurdist humor with efficient delivery. The visual of a self-consuming octopus is genuinely bizarre and memorable.

jokes.text: Am I the only one here who's in horrible pain? / You're the only one who won't shut up about it.
jokes.setup: Character complaining about being in horrible pain
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response suggesting everyone's in pain but only one person complains
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with good timing. The dismissive response is perfectly calibrated for alien characters.

jokes.text: Cool! I'm vomit!
jokes.setup: Character says they feel like they're going to burst from eating too much
jokes.punchline: Another character enthusiastically identifies as vomit
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and economical. The enthusiasm for being vomit is perfectly alien and unexpected.

jokes.text: Homer, what are you doing? / Trying to get a Frisbee off the roof.
jokes.setup: Marge asks what Homer is doing
jokes.punchline: Homer casually explains he's doing something that will clearly be dangerous/problematic
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer setup establishing the running Frisbee gag. Good character integration but predictable structure.

jokes.text: Concussion... diddily... Hemorrhage... Doodily... Injury... Bodily...
jokes.setup: Ned has been injured and is speaking
jokes.punchline: He maintains his characteristic speech pattern even while listing serious injuries
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy. The contrast between serious medical terms and Ned's folksy speech pattern is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Ned, you nearly died... of a brain tumor. Thank goodness that bowling ball knocked it out of your head.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining Ned's near-death experience
jokes.punchline: Bowling ball trauma somehow cured brain cancer
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical logic that's both ridiculous and delivered with medical authority. Good subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: Hey, while you're there, can you get my Frisbee off the ledge? / All right, but I swear to God, this is the very last time.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Ned to get the Frisbee while Ned is clearly in danger
jokes.punchline: Ned's exasperated agreement with religious oath
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Homer's selfishness and Ned's reluctant helpfulness. Sets up future callbacks.

jokes.text: Just because you foresaw Dr. Hibbert's death... doesn't mean you can see into the future. It was just a sinister co-inky-dink.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to rationalize Ned's apparent psychic abilities
jokes.punchline: Homer's mispronunciation of 'coincidence' as 'co-inky-dink'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer malapropism. The made-up word is both endearing and shows his character's limited vocabulary.

jokes.text: Some bullies threw my shoes over a telephone wire... with me in them!
jokes.setup: Reference to common prank of throwing shoes over wires
jokes.punchline: Reveals the kid was still wearing the shoes when thrown
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of a familiar scenario into the absurd. The delayed reveal is perfectly timed and genuinely surprising.

jokes.text: What the Family Circus?
jokes.setup: Homer witnessing another death prediction come true
jokes.punchline: Homer substitutes innocent comic strip name for profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant minced oath using Family Circus. Very quotable and shows The Simpsons' meta-awareness of comics culture.

jokes.text: Do me! Do me!
jokes.setup: Homer eager to know how he'll die
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic phrasing is unintentionally suggestive
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that fits Homer's character but relies on accidental double entendre rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: So, what'd I die of? Too much happiness? Naked girl avalanche?
jokes.setup: Homer asking about his death prediction
jokes.punchline: His fantasy death scenarios reveal his priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - even his death fantasies are about pleasure. 'Naked girl avalanche' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: In this neighborhood, who hasn't?
jokes.setup: Ned mentions having a vision of shooting Homer
jokes.punchline: Lisa casually implies everyone has considered shooting Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy delivered with Lisa's characteristic deadpan. Perfectly captures how annoying Homer is to everyone.

jokes.text: Not in the face.
jokes.setup: Family motto about being shot
jokes.punchline: The family has accepted shooting as inevitable, only caring about location
jokes.explanation: Darkly hilarious family motto. The casual acceptance of violence is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Well, sometimes there's three.
jokes.setup: Homer correcting himself about there being one God
jokes.punchline: Confused reference to the Trinity concept
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's confused understanding of religion. Simple but effective character comedy.

jokes.text: Quit whacking my barrel! / Never!
jokes.setup: Homer hitting the gun barrel to make Ned shoot
jokes.punchline: Homer's defiant refusal to stop despite danger
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stubborness in the face of obvious danger. Physical comedy enhanced by dialogue.

jokes.text: Today is Lenny's birthday, and they're having ice cream cake. / But ice cream cake!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he can't avoid the nuclear plant
jokes.punchline: Ice cream cake trumps potential nuclear disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities. The repetition of 'ice cream cake' with emphasis shows his simple pleasures overriding reason.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you know, you got to take the bitter with the sweet.
jokes.setup: Ned saying Homer will blow up the whole town
jokes.punchline: Lenny's casual acceptance of total annihilation as 'bitter'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement treating nuclear annihilation as a minor inconvenience. Perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Press... destruct button. / Don't do it! Don't do it! You'll kill everyone! / Do it... do it! Kill everyone!
jokes.setup: Ned trying to prevent Homer from pressing destruct button
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal monologue encouraging himself to destroy everything
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's id winning over any sense of responsibility. The internal encouragement to 'kill everyone' is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Sure is taking me a long time to reach this button.
jokes.setup: Dramatic slow-motion approach to button
jokes.punchline: Homer commenting on the pacing of his own dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy breaking the fourth wall. Homer recognizing the artificial drama of his situation.

jokes.text: Everything's coming up Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer has successfully blown up Springfield
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Everything's coming up Milhouse' while celebrating destruction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established catchphrase. Homer's satisfaction with destroying everything is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, the joke's on you, smart guy. The garage made it to Heaven, too.
jokes.setup: Marge angry at Homer for destroying town to avoid cleaning garage
jokes.punchline: The garage somehow exists in Heaven too
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion - Homer can't escape his responsibilities even in death. Marge gets the last word.

jokes.text: Hmm, I wonder if I could blow up Heaven?
jokes.setup: Homer stuck with garage duty even in Heaven
jokes.punchline: Homer considers destroying Heaven rather than do chores
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Homer's laziness. The idea of destroying Heaven to avoid chores is brilliantly extreme.

jokes.text: You're not the boss of me.
jokes.setup: God warning Homer about three-bean salad
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish defiance toward the Almighty
jokes.explanation: Homer's refusal to accept any authority, even divine. Childish but in character.

jokes.text: What's this? A doorknob. Good show.
jokes.setup: Dr. Bartley as easily amazed sidekick
jokes.punchline: He's impressed by the most mundane observation
jokes.explanation: Good parody of Watson-type sidekicks who are amazed by obvious deductions. Economical character establishment.

jokes.text: Lock him up till we find someone darker, boys.
jokes.setup: Police blaming crime on ethnic minority
jokes.punchline: Openly admitting they'll just find someone even more marginalized
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of Victorian-era racism and police incompetence. Effective but potentially problematic humor.

jokes.text: We British sure eat crap.
jokes.setup: Apu listing disgusting British foods
jokes.punchline: Direct commentary on British cuisine quality
jokes.explanation: Standard British food joke. Economical but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: All kettles and biscuits.
jokes.setup: Cockney character describing suspicious person as 'mad'
jokes.punchline: Using British objects as nonsensical description of madness
jokes.explanation: Creative Cockney slang that sounds authentic while being complete nonsense. Good character voice work.

jokes.text: Cor, blimey, skip to me loo.
jokes.setup: Cockney witness describing what she saw
jokes.punchline: Random string of British expressions
jokes.explanation: Lazy stereotypical British dialogue. More cliché than clever, relies on accent humor.

jokes.text: I shall never grow up. / Uh, in jail you will.
jokes.setup: Peter Pan character declaring they'll never grow up
jokes.punchline: Prison will force maturation through trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on Peter Pan mythology. The matter-of-fact delivery of prison trauma as education is effectively grim.

jokes.text: You may examine my curios, but do not touch my oddities. Use your judgment with my gewgaws.
jokes.setup: Shop owner giving browsing instructions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate synonyms for 'stuff' with unclear distinctions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay. The arbitrary rules for different categories of objects is perfectly Comic Book Guy.

jokes.text: I just told the natives they were having sex the wrong way.
jokes.setup: Missionaries being cooked by natives
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of sexual colonialism
jokes.explanation: Dark colonial satire delivered matter-of-factly. Effective but potentially uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: I'm alive. I'm gonna go to America. I'll be in pictures. You'll see.
jokes.setup: Victorian character drinking life potion
jokes.punchline: Classic immigrant American dream speech
jokes.explanation: Standard immigrant cliché delivered earnestly. Somewhat predictable but fits the period piece format.

jokes.text: Burns? The evil industrialist who makes coal out of babies?
jokes.setup: Learning Burns bought the swords
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning his infant coal business
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation of Burns' evil. 'Makes coal out of babies' is memorably horrible and fits Victorian industrial period.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, heads up there, buddy. A rat's gnawin' off your toe.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation in opium den
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning horrific rat attack as minor concern
jokes.explanation: Good opium den atmosphere humor. The casual tone about horrific injury fits the drugged setting.

jokes.text: Then I sold them all for opium. Oh, how I wish I had them back... to sell for more opium!
jokes.setup: Burns explaining he sold valuable swords for drugs
jokes.punchline: His regret is only about having less to sell for more drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns logic - his regret isn't about the drugs, but about maximizing drug acquisition. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Opium rules!
jokes.setup: Homer getting dragged away from opium den
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic endorsement while being arrested
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer enthusiasm for anything pleasurable. Simple but fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: It's the only way I can get Ralphie to go to sleep. / I can make opi-ohs!
jokes.setup: Chief explaining why he's at opium den with his child
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent mispronunciation of opium as cereal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about child endangerment softened by Ralph's innocent wordplay. Very quotable.

jokes.text: It was five minutes ago, you twit.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on body being bloated and rotten
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction about the timeline
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan correction of Victorian medical examination tropes. Lisa's directness cuts through the pompous atmosphere.

jokes.text: And I've never known the pleasures of a woman... or a proper eating apple.
jokes.setup: Character confessing lack of romantic experience
jokes.punchline: Equating sexual inexperience with never eating good fruit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy line - the equation of romantic and culinary inexperience is brilliantly pathetic and specific.

jokes.text: Hey, lots of people like eel pie. / Yeah? Well, how many of them have muttonchops?
jokes.setup: Inspector defending eel pie as common food
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out the specific physical evidence
jokes.explanation: Solid detective story logic. Lisa's deduction is both obvious and overlooked by the inspector.

jokes.text: Why can't you be more like Eddie? He never says a word against me. / That's 'cause you cut out his tongue.
jokes.setup: Boss praising one employee's loyalty
jokes.punchline: Loyalty is due to physical mutilation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace dynamics. The casual reveal of torture as management technique is effectively shocking.

jokes.text: I wanted to come up with a case even you couldn't solve.
jokes.setup: Inspector revealed as murderer
jokes.punchline: His motivation was creating an unsolvable case
jokes.explanation: Good villain motivation - professional pride leads to mass murder. The logic is twisted but consistent.

jokes.text: Remember me for my police work and not the murders!
jokes.setup: Murderous inspector trying to escape
jokes.punchline: His concern for his professional reputation despite being a serial killer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing misplaced priorities. The request for legacy management while fleeing justice is absurd.

jokes.text: This comically large capsule has lifetime supplies of niacin, thiamin and riboflavin-glavin.
jokes.setup: Frink describing a vitamin pill
jokes.punchline: Made-up vitamin name 'riboflavin-glavin' in Frink's characteristic style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink speech pattern with his made-up scientific terms. Character-specific wordplay that's immediately recognizable.

jokes.text: Shrink ray? Oh, there's always something else to buy.
jokes.setup: Frink mentioning need for shrink ray
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical concern about hidden costs
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge practicality cutting through sci-fi nonsense. Good domestic realism humor.

jokes.text: Now, now don't worry, my dear woman. She will be rocked gently to sleep by the stomach acid.
jokes.setup: Frink trying to comfort worried mother
jokes.punchline: Describing acid dissolution as peaceful bedtime routine
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as comfort. The euphemistic description of acid death as gentle sleep is effectively disturbing.

jokes.text: And, uh, while you're in there, grab as much cancer as you can.
jokes.setup: Burns instructing the rescue team
jokes.punchline: Casual request to collect cancer while saving baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns evil - treating cancer as a collectible item. The casual delivery makes it more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: Screw you, doc! I fly from the gut.
jokes.setup: Frink giving scientific navigation advice
jokes.punchline: Homer's anti-intellectual navigation philosophy
jokes.explanation: Homer rejecting expertise in favor of instinct. 'Fly from the gut' is perfect Homer philosophy.

jokes.text: Screw you, doc! I fly from the gut.
jokes.setup: Scientific instruction about which vein to enter
jokes.punchline: Homer rejecting science in favor of instinct with aviation metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Homer anti-intellectualism. The phrase 'fly from the gut' is memorably nonsensical.

jokes.text: Has science ever kissed a woman? or won the Super Bowl? or put a man on the moon?
jokes.setup: Homer defending his anti-science stance
jokes.punchline: Ironically citing space program as achievement not done by science
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic failure. The moon landing example perfectly shows his inability to connect science with achievements.

jokes.text: Help me, science!
jokes.setup: Homer immediately needing help after rejecting science
jokes.punchline: Desperate appeal to what he just dismissed
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback showing Homer's hypocrisy. The quick reversal and desperate tone nail it.

jokes.text: Oh, boy, are they retro! It's a fun design by R. Crumb, who was friends with Harvey Pekar.
jokes.setup: Warning about dangerous retroviruses
jokes.punchline: Frink getting distracted by design aesthetics and comic history
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about cartoon aesthetics. Frink's academic tangent during emergency is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Marge, that's what turns a mediocre voyage... into a fantastic voyage!
jokes.setup: Marge questioning her revealing swimsuit
jokes.punchline: Homer's reference to the movie 'Fantastic Voyage' while objectifying his wife
jokes.explanation: Good movie reference with Homer's typical priorities. The title drop is earned by the parody context.

jokes.text: There's a whole rack of 'em over there. I only ate two.
jokes.setup: Someone asking where Homer got a rib
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admitting to eating Burns's ribs with restraint
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor elevated by Homer's pride in his 'restraint.' The casual cannibalism is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Learning is cool!
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining body's information superhighway
jokes.punchline: Bart's uncharacteristic enthusiasm for education
jokes.explanation: Bart temporarily embracing education, which is amusing for his anti-intellectual character.

jokes.text: Well, that hurt like hell.
jokes.setup: Homer hitting all buttons causing Burns pain
jokes.punchline: Burns's matter-of-fact response to extreme agony
jokes.explanation: Simple understatement humor. Burns's deadpan delivery of obvious pain is characteristic but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! This is the worst Halloween Horror we've ever faced.
jokes.setup: Family trapped in dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking fourth wall to reference the show's anthology format
jokes.explanation: Strong meta-humor acknowledging the anthology structure. Homer's awareness of being in a Halloween special is unexpected.

jokes.text: Boy, when God closes a door, He opens a window.
jokes.setup: Homer about to die inside Burns
jokes.punchline: Finding optimism in discovering marshmallow while facing death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer optimism finding divine providence in finding food. His priorities never waver even facing death.

jokes.text: Cut me some slack. I'm a widow.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about Marge's navigation through boogers
jokes.punchline: Marge matter-of-factly claiming widow status while Homer is still alive
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Marge prematurely accepting Homer's death. Her matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Cut me some slack. I'm a widow.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if Marge had to fly through boogers
jokes.punchline: Marge using recent widowhood to excuse gross exit
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's death delivered matter-of-factly. Marge using tragedy to excuse bodily function humor.

jokes.text: I say when we're full! You don't wanna piss off a man who can kick you in the crotch... from the inside!
jokes.setup: Homer grown large inside Burns, refusing food
jokes.punchline: Threatening internal violence from his unique position
jokes.explanation: Brilliant threat utilizing Homer's unique situation. The specificity of 'from the inside' makes it memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Cheer up, my chubby inner child. I've got a feeling everything's going to work out fine because...
jokes.setup: Burns trying to comfort the Homer inside him
jokes.punchline: Setting up the musical number with his characteristic condescension
jokes.explanation: Sets up the musical finale while maintaining Burns's patronizing tone. 'Chubby inner child' is perfectly condescending.
